session_label: Roll-up summary: 6 Gemini-recommended new-witness-discovery avenues + Digital Orientalist 2023 residual closure
scope: Synthesis across the 5 per-avenue probe artifacts produced this round (Caucasus main-context, South Asia subagent A, Central Asia/Russia/Yerevan subagent B, Ottoman provincial/Sufi main-context replacing failed subagent C, DO 2023 residual closure subagent D). Documents total reach, witness yield, v2.0 scope additions, methodological findings, and v1.0.1 release-candidate readiness.
parent_context: Builds on v1.0.0 deposit at GitHub HEAD 8baca71. v1.0.0 witness_register.json has 14 active entries + 2 rejected (both Baku-related). This roll-up is the v1.0.1 release candidate batch.
geopolitical_caveat: Iranian wartime intermittent shutdowns affect Iranian portal reachability (NLAI, Tehran U utdlib, Astan-e Quds, Malek, Majles, lib.eshia.ir, ketab.ir). Az/Geo/Am Caucasus portals are not affected. VPN was OFF during this round; results reflect direct US/UK routing.

subagent B with WebFetch + WebSearch + scholarly trail triangulation

session_totals.total_queries_executed: approximately 100+ across all artifacts (Caucasus ~10, South Asia ~25, Central Asia/Russia/Yerevan ~20, Ottoman ~10, DO 2023 ~33)
session_totals.v1_register_impact: ZERO new witnesses. 14-entry baseline unchanged. One augmentation recommended to matenadaran_965 (Gazaryan provenance + 1959 YSU-Matenadaran consolidation + Hüseyinzadə Pedagogical-Institute clarification).

key_findings_by_avenue.1_caucasus: All 5 Az/Geo/Am popular-history Hagigi MS locations map onto existing v1.0 register entries (bl_or_9493, suleymaniye_fatih_3808, matenadaran_965, tehran_university_8198). manuscript.az 'newly acquired from Süleymaniye' is a copy/microfilm of Fatih 3808. manuscript.ge LIVE under VPN-off (was ECONNREFUSED under VPN-on); test-mode operation; Persian Afshar-period scope (EAP1526) confirms post-Cihanşah holdings only. Matenadaran has no online search.
key_findings_by_avenue.4_south_asia: All 6 portals EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE or BLOCKED. 8 of 14 grepped printed catalogues delivered strong negatives with healthy OCR (Bankipore Persian Vols II/III/XIII via Khuda Bakhsh, Ivanow 1924 + Mirza Ashraf Ali via Asiatic Society Kolkata). Bonus v2.0 chancery finding: Bodleian MS Pers d. 84 (Vol III entry 2711) is an insha collection containing one Cihansah firman to Sultan Muhammad II ~1455. Residual gaps: Salar Jung Vol IV (highest-yield window, 500MB PDF not retrieved); NAMAMI/Kriti Sampada (Indian-IP needed).
key_findings_by_avenue.2_3_central_asia_russia_yerevan: All 8 portals EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE via web. Major triangulation finding: Az Wikipedia 'NYC Gazaryan -> Yerevan University -> Hüseynzade publication' narrative CONFLATES BL Or 9493's London examination with the Yerevan-held codex. Hüseyinzadə was at Yerevan Pedagogical Institute (NOT YSU); discovery 1961; first publication Yerevan 1966. Gazaryan-bequest codex is most plausibly Matenadaran MS 965 itself, via 1959 YSU-to-Matenadaran institutional consolidation per Iranica MATENADARAN. Bonus v2.0 chancery lead: Kostikyan Persian Decrees vols III (2005) + IV (2008) parallel to asnad.org. Tatar/Kazan/Bashkort portals all unresolvable/defunct.
key_findings_by_avenue.5_6_provincial_ottoman_sufi: All 4 site-restricted Google sweeps returned ZERO Cihanşah / Hakîkî / Karakoyunlu hits on yazmalar.gov.tr, katalog.isam.org.tr, mkutup.gov.tr, yazmaeserler.diyanet.gov.tr. Consistent with v1.0's TEIS Yesevi 5-witness Cihanşah roster being saturated via Islamisation DB (islam-anatolia.ac.uk). Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi homonym risk concentrated at Bursa + Bektaşi tekke (Konya Hacı Bektaş BY0000010729 already in v1.0 with disambiguation methodology). Subagent C lost to API overload; main-context replacement is shallow site-restricted; residual gap is Playwright cert-ignore pass at yazmalar.gov.tr.
key_findings_by_avenue.do2023_residual_closure: 4 portals (MENA-DOC, HathiTrust, DLME, OPenn) EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE. Methodological yield: query endpoint discoveries (MENA-DOC backend at hallelit.gbv.de Ha:Lit SPA; DLME Blacklight URL signatures; OPenn = Google site-search relay; HathiTrust UA-/Cloudflare-walled). One new homonym FP class documented: Mughal Shah Jahan (r. 1628-1658) vs Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu (d. 1467). All 28 DO 2023 article resources now formally closed.

augmentations_no_count_change.content: Pre-1959 institutional history: Az-language popular-history accounts (Az Wikipedia + Azertag.az + Azadliq.info) describe the codex as bequeathed by NYC Armenian collector Arutyun Gazaryan to 'Yerevan University' and discovered there by Lətif Hüseynzadə (Yerevan Pedagogical Institute, not YSU). Discovery 1961; first publication Yerevan 1966. Per Iranica MATENADARAN article, in 1959 the manuscript holdings of multiple Yerevan institutions including YSU were consolidated into Matenadaran. The 'Yerevan University' attribution in popular-history accounts is the pre-1959 institutional location of the codex now held as Matenadaran MS 965. The Az Wiki narrative additionally conflates this provenance with BL Or 9493's separate London examination; these are two different codices.
augmentations_no_count_change.rationale: Adds documented institutional-history context to an existing register entry. Increases scholarly defensibility of the matenadaran_965 entry by clarifying the popular-history-to-institutional-record mapping.

v1_1_register_actions_recommended.no_new_witnesses: Zero entries added to witnesses[]. by_verification array remains: verified=8, verified_with_attribution_caveat=1, candidate_probably_non_cihansah=1, lost_witness_attested_only=1, candidate_probably_yusuf_hakiki_or_other_homonym=1, rejected=2 [sum=14, unchanged].

v2_0_backlog_additions.content: Bodleian MS Pers d. 84 (Vol III entry 2711) is an insha collection containing one Cihansah firman to Sultan Muhammad II ~1455. Adds to asnad.org corpus (21-23 docs) for v2.0 chancery scope.
Kostikyan, Persian Decrees vol III (2005) + vol IV (2008). Yerevan-published. Parallel chancery-document corpus to asnad.org. Paywalled on Academia.edu; ILL or direct purchase needed.

Diyanet yazmaeserler.diyanet.gov.tr holds [er-Risâle fî esrâri'l-hurûf] (id=931, materialType=YE), a Hurufi text. Per v1.0 Nesîmî-adjacency mecmûʿa heuristic, Diyanet Hurufi-text holdings are a v2.0 mecmûʿa-mining surface.

Milli Kütüphane dijital-kutuphane.mkutup.gov.tr/authority/papertypelist endpoint enumerates paper-type metadata for the manuscript holdings. Useful for v2.0 codicology-augmentation work; integrates with project's separate decorated-paper corpus.

homonym_matrix_expansion_recommended.purpose: Document false-positive name-collision classes encountered across all 5 avenue probes. Goes into a future v2.0 / 'disambiguation_taxonomy.json' artifact or as an augmentation to data/search_keys.json.

methodological_findings_consolidated: VPN routing affects portal reachability signatures: manuscript.az returned socket-closed under VPN-on, HTTP 200 under VPN-off; manuscript.ge returned ECONNREFUSED under VPN-on, REACHABLE_TEST_MODE under VPN-off. VPN-routed false-negatives are a documented hazard for Az/Geo/Am portal probing.
Site-restricted Google indexing is a Playwright substitute with a known false-negative class for JS-rendered SPA catalogues; suggestive but not definitive of 'no record exists'.

Subagent context-isolation reduces main-context burn but introduces a narrow-waist failure mode: API overload at the final summary write can lose 32+ minutes / 60+ tool-uses of probe work even when the actual probes succeeded. Mitigation: instruct subagents to write incremental Bash-append checkpoints, not single final write.

Popular-history Azerbaijani-language provenance accounts (Az Wikipedia + Azadliq + Azertag) conflate institutional consolidation events (1959 YSU-Matenadaran transfer) and can manufacture apparent 'multiple witnesses' from a single codex at different institutional moments. Cross-check against Iranica MATENADARAN before treating popular-history MS locations as distinct.

Islamisation DB (islam-anatolia.ac.uk) is the canonical Anatolian Cihanşah witness-discovery surface. Turkish public-facing portals (yazmalar, ISAM, Milli Kütüphane, Diyanet) appear to host the same records under different institutional fronts without adding new Cihanşah witnesses in site-restricted indexing.

Newly-acquired-manuscripts logs on institutional websites are an under-exploited probe surface: AMEA manuscript.az main page acquisition-table directly mentioned 'Jahanshah Hagigi (1405-1467)' obtained from Süleymaniye, providing a direct institutional-self-disclosure of a register-existing entry.

OCR quality as confidence modifier: subagent A's distinction between 'strong negative with healthy OCR' (Bankipore Vols II/III/XIII grep-clean at 7-8/13 QA hits) and 'degraded OCR residual gap' (Rampur 0-1/14) is the right epistemic posture for printed-catalogue grep-mining.
